candidate_problems.text: What exactly is the explanatory bridge from 'stable iterated self-modeling and internal negotiation' to phenomenal feel rather than only functional self-coordination? The conjecture identifies structural conditions for an enduring point of view, but it remains open why those conditions should generate subjective experience at all instead of merely better integrated control, making this the central hard explanatory tension.

Which architectural features are necessary and sufficient for the proposed repeated internal game: recurrence, memory depth, global observability, cross-module prediction, error correction, bargaining among subsystems, or some specific combination? Without a principled criterion, the conjecture risks being too flexible and cannot sharply distinguish conscious from nonconscious systems.

How can the conjecture be empirically discriminated from nearby theories such as global workspace, higher-order thought, predictive processing, recurrent processing, and integrated information? Many of its predicted correlates—temporal integration, reportability, working memory, global availability—overlap with existing accounts, so a key open problem is to derive novel signatures unique to the iterated-game view.
